hero.description: Mineral systems for watertightness and pressure resistance – for dams, reservoirs, water treatment plants and marine infrastructure. Engineered for W20, 15 Bar and long service life even underwater.

diagnostics.title: WATER IS UNFORGIVING. GEONYX HYDRO IS NOT.
diagnostics.subtitle: 8 hydraulic engineering scenarios where standard concrete is slowly losing the battle.

d1.title: UNDERWATER REPAIR WITHOUT DEWATERING
d1.problem: Draining a dam, reservoir or basin costs millions and halts the operation of critical infrastructure.
d1.solution: GEONYX HYDRO cures underwater without washout. Divers perform local repairs on operating structures – with minimal downtime.

d2.title: HYDROSTATIC PRESSURE UP TO 15 BAR
d2.problem: Water under pressure (tens of metres of head) forces through pores and micro-cracks, compromising the structure.
d2.solution: GEONYX HYDRO delivers Class W20 performance and resistance to positive pressure up to 15 Bar – no breakthrough, no layer delamination.

d3.title: CAVITATION AND HIGH-VELOCITY FLOW
d3.problem: Vacuum bubbles and turbulent flow in spillways, channels and sluices erode concrete and produce deep pitting.
d3.solution: Extremely dense and smooth surface with low abrasion (AR ≤ 0.5), resistant to cavitation and mechanical wear from flow.

d4.title: BIOGENIC CORROSION IN SEWERS AND WWTP
d4.problem: Bacteria in sewage convert gases into aggressive acids that attack concrete above the waterline.
d4.solution: Mineral immunity to biogenic corrosion, combined where required with GEONYX CHEM – protecting both wet and gas zones in a single solution.

d5.title: CHLORIDE ATTACK AND MARINE ENVIRONMENT
d5.problem: Sea salt penetrates through pores, reaches the reinforcement and accelerates corrosion of coastal structures and quays.
d5.solution: Dense mineral barrier that limits chloride migration and extends the service life of reinforced elements in marine environments.

d6.title: LEACHING AND "LEOPARD SKIN"
d6.problem: Soft or demineralised water dissolves lime from concrete, causing staining, porosity and loss of strength.
d6.solution: Geopolymer structure with no free lime – nothing to leach out; the surface remains stable and dense.

d7.title: DRINKING WATER SAFETY
d7.problem: Standard epoxy coatings can release monomers and solvents into drinking water – unacceptable for operators and regulators.
d7.solution: GEONYX HYDRO is a mineral system certified for contact with drinking water to DVGW/BS standards – no organic toxic components.

d8.title: ABRASION FROM SILT AND SAND
d8.problem: Water laden with sand and silt acts as a continuous abrasive on the walls and floors of channels and basins.
d8.solution: Surface hardness up to Mohs 9 with very low volume loss – significantly more resistant than standard concrete and renders.

anatomy.sectionSubtitle: Interactive layer model – from the substrate to the working surface.

h1.title: 1. CONCRETE SUBSTRATE
h1.subtitle: Saturated Substrate
h1.desc: Existing reinforced concrete structure, cleaned by high-pressure hydroblasting (2500 bar) to open the capillaries.

h2.title: 2. CRYSTALLINE PRIMER
h2.subtitle: Active Penetration
h2.desc: Active primer that penetrates deep into the concrete pores and crystallises on contact with moisture, blocking capillary rise.

h3.title: 3. HYDRO-MORTAR
h3.subtitle: Fiber Reinforced Body
h3.desc: Primary structural layer, fibre-reinforced. Withstands hydrostatic pressure up to 20 Bar (W20) and is watertight throughout its mass.

h4.title: 4. SMOOTH FINISH
h4.subtitle: Hydraulic Flow Surface
h4.desc: Smooth surface membrane to reduce friction and increase hydraulic flow rate. Resistant to abrasion and chemicals.

engineering.title: LABORATORY TESTS AND FIELD TRIALS
engineering.subtitle: Independent tests in accredited laboratories – full protocols available in TDS.
engineering.block1note: GEONYX HYDRO achieves Class W20 – a water column of 200 metres without breakthrough.
engineering.block2note: Comparison of hydrostatic pressure resistance across different systems.
engineering.block3note: Difference in water losses over 20 years without/with HYDRO protection.

r1.title: UNDERWATER APPLICATION
r1.desc: Curing under active water – divers can work even in wet or partially submerged zones.

r2.title: CLASS W20 / 15 BAR
r2.desc: Water impermeability Class W20 and resistance to both positive and negative hydrostatic pressure.

r3.title: DRINKING WATER (DVGW/BS)
r3.desc: Certified for contact with drinking water – no organic monomers, no toxic leaching.

r4.title: MARINE AND COASTAL PROTECTION
r4.desc: Resistance to chlorides, sea salt and sulphates – for port structures and quay walls.

r5.title: ZERO LOSSES (NRW)
r5.desc: Joint-free and dense system minimises water losses in reservoirs, dams and distribution networks.

financial.title: WHEN WATER IS AN ASSET, NOT A PROBLEM
financial.subtitle: Comparison of losses and costs in protected vs unprotected hydraulic structures.
financial.quote: AN UNPROTECTED DAM LOSES WATER. AND MONEY.

roi.title: INVESTMENT AUDIT
roi.subtitle: 25-year life cycle comparison for hydraulic engineering assets.

applications.title: WHERE IT IS USED
applications.subtitle: Structures with high risk of water infiltration, pressure and hydrochemical aggression.

a1.title: Dams and Hydropower Plants
a1.desc: Lining of dam bodies, intake towers and gate chambers.

a2.title: Water Treatment Plants
a2.desc: Bio-basins, clarifiers and anaerobic reactors (biogenic corrosion).

a3.title: Drinking Water Reservoirs
a3.desc: Pressure tanks and water towers (DVGW/BS certified).

a4.title: Marine Infrastructure
a4.desc: Quays, breakwaters, underwater tunnels and platforms.

a5.title: Channels and Sluices
a5.desc: Hydraulic protective linings against cavitation and flow abrasion.

a6.title: Mine Tailings Facilities
a6.desc: Watertight impoundments for containment of process liquids.

a7.title: Pumping Stations
a7.desc: Wet wells and tunnels carrying aggressive wastewater.

a8.title: Tunnels and Metro
a8.desc: Vaults and walls subject to groundwater ingress.

process.title: EXECUTION PROCESS
process.subtitle: Precision execution under active water and underwater conditions.

p1.title: Hydro-audit of the structure
p1.desc: Analysis of pressure, leaks, water chemical profile and level of biogenic activity.

p2.title: Selection of HYDRO configuration
p2.desc: System selection – crystalline primer, HYDRO-MORTAR, SMOOTH FINISH or combination with CHEM.

p3.title: Hydroblasting
p3.desc: Water jet at 2500 bar to open pores and remove weak zones.

p4.title: Crystalline primer
p4.desc: Application of crystalline primer – activates upon moisture contact and blocks capillary flow.

p5.title: Application and sealing
p5.desc: Application of HYDRO-MORTAR and SMOOTH FINISH per the technical protocol – including underwater where required.

p6.title: Hydraulic test and documentation
p6.desc: Water impermeability test, protocols, TDS package and warranty card.

offer.title: HYDRO OFFER PACKAGE (B2B / B2G)
offer.desc: Complete documentation for dams, reservoirs, WWTP and marine infrastructure.
offer.qualityDesc: Compliant with DVGW, BS and Eurocode hydraulic engineering standards.
offer.doc1: TDS – Technical specifications and hydraulic testing
offer.doc6: Tender specification for hydraulic engineering projects

dl4.title: Underwater execution methodology
dl4.sub: Steps, diving requirements and quality control

cta.title: YOUR INFRASTRUCTURE SHOULD NOT LOSE WATER – OR MONEY.
cta.desc: Describe the hydraulic structure and the problem – we will propose a HYDRO configuration, W-class documentation and TCO analysis.

footerBand.label: TECHNOLOGY PARTNER FOR CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE
footerBand.desc: GEONYX HYDRO protects dams, reservoirs and water treatment plants where standard concrete fails.
